name: The Elephant Corridor
description: Authentic Sri Lankan rice and curry in an open-air setting overlooking paddy fields near Sigiriya.
mustTry: Rice and curry platter with jackfruit, dhal, and pol sambol

name: Cafe Chill Ella
description: A laid-back hillside cafe with hammocks, mountain views, and great smoothie bowls.
mustTry: Avocado toast with egg and fresh passion fruit smoothie

name: Empire Cafe
description: A charming cafe inside Kandy's colonial-era Central Market with traditional Sri Lankan snacks and good coffee.
mustTry: Kottu roti with spicy chicken and a lime tea

name: The Wild Kitchen
description: Outdoor bush dining at Yala, featuring fresh catch from the nearby coast paired with jungle ambience.
mustTry: Grilled lobster with coconut sauce and coconut rice

name: The Old Dutch House
description: Fine dining inside a 300-year-old Dutch colonial building within Galle Fort, offering incredible atmosphere and creative cuisine.
mustTry: Devilled prawns with flat bread and sunset cocktails from the terrace

name: Hill Club Nuwara Eliya
description: A historic colonial club established in 1876 offering a formal English dining experience in the Sri Lankan highlands.
mustTry: Traditional English roast dinner with Yorkshire pudding and freshly brewed Ceylon tea

name: Dewmini Roti Shop
description: Famous small family-run spot on Mirissa Beach road serving every variety of roti imaginable at incredibly low prices.
mustTry: Egg roti with pol sambol and fresh coconut water

name: Sithara Restaurant
description: A beloved local spot near the ancient ruins serving generous rice and curry spreads to pilgrims and travelers.
mustTry: Vegetarian rice and curry with local ash plantain curry
